message:
Revise API harmonization beads: reopen stubs, add testing, link dependencies

- Reopen bd-3p04, bd-3rwt, bd-27f3, bd-1ey4 (were stubs, not complete)
- Reopen bd-h1ai EPIC (children still pending)
- Create bd-39wn: wa robot approve detailed implementation spec
- Create bd-qvbz: Robot workflow subcommands unit + E2E tests
- Link bd-* beads to wa-* implementation beads (relates-to)
- Add blocking dependencies on workflow engine and approval tokens
- Update bead descriptions with clear done/pending status
- Add testing requirements to all reopened beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-12f4","title":"E2E: TUI smoke + interaction logging","description":"## Goal
Validate the TUI end-to-end with scripted interactions and detailed logs.

## Requirements
- Drive a scripted TUI session (pane list, events feed, search, workflow panel) using a deterministic fixture DB.
- Capture session transcript + keypress log + screenshots (or text snapshots) as artifacts.
- Ensure no panics and output remains stable under resize/refresh.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E TUI scenario passes locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include TUI transcript, interaction log, and snapshots.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:40.548004359Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:38.242172171Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12f4","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:38.242094625Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12f4","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:36.104245300Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12f4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:40.564448518Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dnn","title":"Add wa robot why command","description":"## Goal
Add `wa robot why <code>` command for agents to understand error codes and policy denials.

## Rationale
When agents receive errors like `robot.policy_denied` or `robot.require_approval`, they need programmatic access to explanations and remediation steps.

## Implementation
1. Add `RobotCommands::Why { code: String }`
2. Query `wa_core::explanations::get_explanation(code)`
3. Return JSON with: scenario, brief, detailed, suggestions, see_also
4. Create wa-robot-why.json schema

## Response Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "code": "deny.alt_screen",
    "scenario": "Send denied because alt-screen is active",
    "brief": "Pane is in full-screen mode (vim, less, etc.)",
    "detailed": "The pane is currently displaying...",
    "suggestions": ["Exit the full-screen application first", ...],
    "see_also": ["wa policy", "wa status --pane <id>"]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot why deny.alt_screen` returns explanation
- `wa robot why unknown.code` returns error with available codes
- JSON schema validates response","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:14.633050084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:04.476889868Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:04.476841497Z","close_reason":"Implemented: wa robot why command using get_explanation()","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dnn","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:14.663015047Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ey4","title":"Add wa robot workflow abort command","description":"## Goal
Add `wa robot workflow abort <execution_id>` to abort running workflows.

## Implementation
1. Add `WorkflowSubcommand::Abort { execution_id: String, reason: Option<String> }`
2. Update workflow status to "aborted" in DB
3. Return confirmation with final status
4. Create wa-robot-workflow-abort.json schema

## Response Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "execution_id": "robot-handle_compaction-1706000000",
    "aborted": true,
    "previous_status": "running",
    "reason": "User requested abort"
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Aborts running workflow
- Returns error if already completed/aborted
- JSON schema validates response","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:50.188606826Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:27.008190427Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:27.008140133Z","close_reason":"Stubbed: wa robot workflow abort returns not-implemented pending persistent execution tracking","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ey4","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:50.205867871Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1ey4","title":"Add wa robot workflow abort command","description":"# wa robot workflow abort

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-abort.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Implement workflow abort with cleanup and audit trail.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id and optional --reason
2. Validate workflow is abortable (not already completed)
3. Mark as aborting, run cleanup steps
4. Release pane lock
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Abort stops running workflow
- Unit: Cleanup steps run (unless --force)
- Unit: Pane lock released
- Unit: Error code stability (E_ALREADY_COMPLETED, etc.)
- E2E: See bd-qvbz for lifecycle scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Safely aborts running workflows
- [ ] Cleanup steps execute (unless --force)
- [ ] Pane lock released after abort
- [ ] Audit trail records abort with reason
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

 {"id":"bd-1zgb","title":"E2E: reliability hardening scenarios with verbose logs","description":"## Goal
Validate resilience features end-to-end with detailed logging and artifacts.

## Requirements
- Execute fault injection scenarios that trigger circuit breaker, retry, and graceful degradation paths.
- Capture verbose logs (trace/debug) plus key metrics snapshots as artifacts.
- Verify user-facing output is stable and actionable during degraded mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E scenarios pass locally and in CI.
- Failure artifacts include full logs, triggered fault labels, and observed policy outputs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:24.619323361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:47.826611461Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:47.826558241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:44.505086097Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zgb","depends_on_id":"wa-37x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:24.648968364Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-27f3","title":"Add wa robot workflow status command","description":"## Goal
Add `wa robot workflow status <execution_id>` to check workflow execution status.

## Implementation
1. Add `WorkflowSubcommand::Status { execution_id: String }`
2. Query workflow_executions table
3. Return status, current_step, result, error, timestamps
4. Create wa-robot-workflow-status.json schema

## Response Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "execution_id": "robot-handle_compaction-1706000000",
    "workflow_name": "handle_compaction",
    "pane_id": 0,
    "status": "running",
    "current_step": 2,
    "total_steps": 5,
    "started_at": 1706000000000,
    "updated_at": 1706000001000,
    "step_logs": [...]
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns execution status by ID
- Shows step progress
- JSON schema validates response","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:45.493925992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:22.405668415Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:22.405546105Z","close_reason":"Stubbed: wa robot workflow status returns not-implemented pending persistent execution tracking","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-27f3","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:45.523092330Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Query workflow_executions table for execution state.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id parameter
2. Query workflow_executions + workflow_step_log tables
3. Return status, current_step, step_logs, timestamps, error

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Returns execution status by ID
- Unit: Shows step progress accurately
- Unit: Error code stability (E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queries workflow_executions table
- [ ] Returns real-time step progress
- [ ] Includes step_logs array
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

 {"id":"bd-2kl8","title":"Change wa robot workflow to require explicit 'run' subcommand","description":"## Goal
Change `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id>` to `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane_id>` for consistency with planned workflow list/status/abort subcommands.

## Breaking Change
This is a breaking change. Agents using `wa robot workflow` must update to `wa robot workflow run`.

## Implementation
1. Add `WorkflowSubcommand` enum with `Run`, `List`, `Status`, `Abort` variants
2. Change `RobotCommands::Workflow` to take subcommand instead of positional args
3. Update quick-start guide examples
4. Update error messages to reference new syntax

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane_id>` works as before
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id>` shows helpful error pointing to new syntax
- Quick-start guide reflects new command structure","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:08.619598006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:54.068422868Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:54.068365820Z","close_reason":"Implemented: workflow run verb change with subcommand pattern","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kl8","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:08.649391385Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rwq","title":"Rename --unhandled-only flag to --unhandled","description":"## Goal

## Implementation

2. Keep `--unhandled-only` as deprecated alias for backwards compatibility
3. Update quick-start guide

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot events --unhandled` works
- `wa robot events --unhandled-only` still works but is deprecated
- No breaking change for existing users","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:12.005890079Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:59.365561884Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:36:59.365509565Z","close_reason":"Implemented: --unhandled flag with visible_alias for backwards compat","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rwq","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:12.035918241Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-38et","title":"E2E: perf regression smoke with detailed logging","description":"## Goal
Validate performance optimizations end-to-end without regressions, with high-fidelity logs.

## Requirements
- Run a representative high-load scenario (many panes / large transcripts) using existing fixtures.
- Capture timing metrics and verbose logs as artifacts (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS query).
- Assert performance budgets are met and output remains correct.

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E perf smoke passes locally and in CI.
- On failure, artifacts include full logs, timing metrics, and scenario parameters.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:32.127057017Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:42.346832169Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:42.346773919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:43:40.210965063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-38et","depends_on_id":"wa-3g9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:20:32.156661123Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-39wn","title":"wa robot approve: submit approval codes in robot mode","description":"# wa robot approve

## Purpose
Allow agents to submit approval codes programmatically through robot mode. This mirrors the human `wa approve` command but with JSON output suitable for automation.

## Command Interface
```bash
# Submit an approval code
wa robot approve <code>

# Submit with explicit pane context (for fingerprint validation)
wa robot approve <code> --pane 3
```

## JSON Output Schema
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "command": "approve",
  "data": {
    "code": "a1b2c3",
    "valid": true,
    "action_kind": "SendText",
    "pane_id": 3,
    "expires_at": 1737300000000,
    "consumed_at": 1737296400000,
    "action_fingerprint": "sha256:abc123..."
  }
}
```

## Error Cases
- E_APPROVAL_NOT_FOUND: No approval with given code
- E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED: Approval code has expired
- E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED: Approval already used
- E_FINGERPRINT_MISMATCH: Action changed since approval issued
- E_WRONG_PANE: Approval was for different pane

## Implementation Notes
- Query ApprovalStore for the approval token
- Validate workspace scope, expiry, and fingerprint
- Mark approval as consumed on success
- Record in audit trail

## Relationship to Human Command
- `wa approve` (wa-nu4.3.2.12): Human-friendly CLI with colored output
- `wa robot approve` (this bead): JSON output for agent consumption
- Both use the same underlying ApprovalStore

## Testing
- Unit tests: JSON serialization, error code stability
- Integration: Approval consumption works correctly
- E2E: Agent can request approval, human grants via `wa approve`, agent confirms via `wa robot approve`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Approval submission returns structured JSON
- [ ] All error cases return stable error codes
- [ ] Approval consumption is audited
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Works with existing ApprovalStore infrastructure","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:09.929485182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:21.759632515Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-robot","area-safety","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39wn","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:09.973556779Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39wn","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:21.759553917Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39wn","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:51:18.411232480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Validate shell completions, aliases, and progressive disclosure end-to-end with detailed logging.

- Exercise completions and aliases in a scripted shell session with verbose logs enabled.
- Verify help output and verbosity tiers are stable (default/verbose/debug).
- Capture logs, command outputs, and completion snapshots as artifacts on failure.

-{"id":"bd-3p04","title":"Add wa robot approve command","description":"## Goal
Add `wa robot approve <code>` command for agents to submit approval tokens.

## Rationale
When policy returns `robot.require_approval`, agents need to submit the approval code to proceed.

## Implementation
1. Add `RobotCommands::Approve { code: String, for_action: Option<String> }`
2. Validate approval code via `wa_core::approval::validate_approval_token`
3. Return success with action allowed, or error with reason
4. Create wa-robot-approve.json schema

## Response Format
\`\`\`json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "code": "ABC12345",
    "approved": true,
    "action_kind": "send_text",
    "pane_id": 0,
    "expires_at": 1706000000000,
    "remaining_uses": 0
  }
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot approve ABC12345` validates and consumes token
- Invalid/expired codes return appropriate errors
- JSON schema validates response","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:36.255366399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:08.874390044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:08.874340621Z","close_reason":"Stubbed: wa robot approve returns not-implemented pending daemon infrastructure","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p04","depends_on_id":"bd-h1ai","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:21:36.337141267Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3p04","title":"Add wa robot approve command","description":"# wa robot approve

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-approve.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Integrate with ApprovalStore to validate and consume tokens.

## Implementation
1. Accept approval code parameter
2. Query ApprovalStore for token
3. Validate workspace scope, expiry, fingerprint
4. Mark as consumed on success
5. Record in audit trail

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Valid code consumes token and returns success
- Unit: Expired codes return E_APPROVAL_EXPIRED
- Unit: Already-consumed codes return E_APPROVAL_CONSUMED
- Unit: Wrong pane/workspace returns appropriate error
- E2E: Full approval flow (request → grant → consume)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validates approval codes via ApprovalStore
- [ ] Consumes token on success
- [ ] All error cases return stable error codes
- [ ] Audit trail records approval consumption
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

-{"id":"bd-h1ai","title":"EPIC: Robot Mode API Harmonization","description":"## Goal
Harmonize the wa robot mode API for consistency, ergonomics, and agent-friendliness.

## Background
API review identified several inconsistencies:
- `wa robot workflow` uses implicit "run" verb conflicting with planned list/status/abort subcommands
- Flag naming inconsistent (`--unhandled-only` vs `--unhandled`)
- Missing parity commands (`why`, `approve`) needed for agent self-recovery
- Missing JSON schemas for planned commands

## Scope
1. **Breaking Changes**
   - Change `wa robot workflow <name> <pane>` → `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane>`
   - Rename `--unhandled-only` → `--unhandled`

2. **New Commands**
   - `wa robot why <code>` - explain errors/denials
   - `wa robot approve <code>` - submit approval tokens
   - `wa robot workflow list` - list available workflows
   - `wa robot workflow status <id>` - check execution status
   - `wa robot workflow abort <id>` - abort running workflow

3. **JSON Schemas**
   - wa-robot-workflow-list.json
   - wa-robot-workflow-status.json
   - wa-robot-workflow-abort.json
   - wa-robot-why.json
   - wa-robot-approve.json

## Acceptance Criteria
- All robot commands follow consistent naming patterns
- All commands have corresponding JSON schemas
- Quick-start guide updated with new command structure
- Tests pass for all new/modified commands","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:20:47.000012947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:46.118165419Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T18:37:46.118116417Z","close_reason":"EPIC complete: API harmonization implemented with all subcommands","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

## Goal
Harmonize the wa robot mode API for consistency, ergonomics, and agent-friendliness.

## Current State

### Completed (CLI Structure)
✅ Change `wa robot workflow <name>` → `wa robot workflow run <name>`
✅ Add workflow subcommands: list, status, abort (CLI stubs)
✅ Rename `--unhandled-only` → `--unhandled` (with backwards-compat alias)
✅ Add `wa robot why <code>` command (fully implemented)
✅ Add `wa robot approve <code>` command (CLI stub)
✅ Create JSON schemas for all new commands
✅ Update quick-start guide with new syntax

### Pending (Full Implementation)

❌ bd-27f3: workflow status - needs workflow_executions table
❌ bd-1ey4: workflow abort - needs workflow_executions table
❌ bd-3p04: approve - needs ApprovalStore integration

- wa-4vx.8.9: Approval tokens (for approve)

## Testing
- bd-qvbz: Robot workflow subcommands unit + E2E tests
- wa-4vx.7.8: Robot mode tests (envelope stability)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All robot commands return real data (not stubs)
- [ ] All commands have unit tests
- [ ] E2E tests pass with detailed logging
- [ ] JSON schemas validate all outputs","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:20:47.000012947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:55:43.623902046Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-h1ai","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Child beads reopened - full implementation pending infrastructure dependencies","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:54:29Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-qvbz","title":"Robot workflow subcommands: unit + E2E tests with detailed logging","description":"# Robot workflow subcommands testing

## Purpose
Comprehensive testing for the harmonized workflow subcommand structure:
- `wa robot workflow run <name> <pane>`
- `wa robot workflow list`
- `wa robot workflow status <id>`
- `wa robot workflow abort <id>`
- `wa robot events --unhandled` (alias for --unhandled-only)

## Unit Tests

### CLI Parsing Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_run_subcommand_parsing() {
    // Old syntax should fail
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_err(), "Implicit run should be rejected");
    
    // New syntax should work
    let result = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "workflow", "run", "handle_compaction", "3"]);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}

#[test]
fn test_unhandled_flag_alias() {
    // Both flags should parse to same value
    let result1 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled"]);
    let result2 = parse_args(&["wa", "robot", "events", "--unhandled-only"]);
    assert_eq!(result1.unhandled_only, result2.unhandled_only);
}
```

### JSON Output Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_list_json_schema() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "list"]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-list.json");
    
    // Verify required fields
    let data: Value = serde_json::from_str(&output)?;
    assert!(data["data"]["workflows"].is_array());
    assert!(data["data"]["total"].is_number());
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_status_json_schema() {
    // Start a workflow first
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", &exec_id]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-status.json");
    
    // Verify status enum values
    let status = &output["data"]["status"];
    assert!(["pending", "running", "waiting", "completed", "aborted", "failed"]
        .contains(&status.as_str().unwrap()));
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_abort_json_schema() {
    let exec_id = start_test_workflow();
    
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id, "--reason", "test"]);
    validate_against_schema(&output, "wa-robot-workflow-abort.json");
    
    assert!(output["data"]["aborted"].as_bool().unwrap());
}
```

### Error Code Stability Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_workflow_not_found_error() {
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "status", "nonexistent-id"]);
    assert_eq!(output["error"]["code"], "E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND");
}

#[test]
fn test_workflow_already_completed_abort() {
    let exec_id = run_and_complete_workflow();
    let output = execute_robot(&["workflow", "abort", &exec_id]);
    assert_eq!(output["data"]["error_reason"], "already_completed");
}
```

## E2E Tests

### Scenario: Workflow List Discovery
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_list.sh

set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE="$ARTIFACT_DIR/workflow_list.log"

log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Starting workflow list E2E test"

# List workflows
OUTPUT=$(wa robot workflow list)
log "Raw output: $OUTPUT"

# Validate JSON
echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e '.ok == true' || { log "FAIL: ok != true"; exit 1; }
echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e '.data.workflows | length > 0' || { log "FAIL: no workflows"; exit 1; }

# Check known workflows exist
WORKFLOWS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r '.data.workflows[].name')
log "Found workflows: $WORKFLOWS"

for EXPECTED in "handle_compaction" "handle_usage_limits"; do
    if echo "$WORKFLOWS" | grep -q "$EXPECTED"; then
        log "✓ Found expected workflow: $EXPECTED"
    else
        log "FAIL: Missing expected workflow: $EXPECTED"
        exit 1
    fi
done

log "✓ PASS: workflow list E2E test"
```

### Scenario: Workflow Run → Status → Abort Flow
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_workflow_lifecycle.sh

set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE="$ARTIFACT_DIR/workflow_lifecycle.log"
PANE_ID="${TEST_PANE_ID:-0}"

log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Starting workflow lifecycle E2E test"

# Start a workflow
log "Starting workflow..."
RUN_OUTPUT=$(wa robot workflow run handle_compaction "$PANE_ID" --dry-run 2>&1 || true)
log "Run output: $RUN_OUTPUT"

# Extract execution ID (if available)
EXEC_ID=$(echo "$RUN_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.data.execution_id // empty')

if [ -n "$EXEC_ID" ]; then
    log "Got execution ID: $EXEC_ID"
    
    # Check status
    log "Checking status..."
    STATUS_OUTPUT=$(wa robot workflow status "$EXEC_ID")
    log "Status output: $STATUS_OUTPUT"
    
    STATUS=$(echo "$STATUS_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.data.status')
    log "Current status: $STATUS"
    
    # Abort if running
    if [ "$STATUS" = "running" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "waiting" ]; then
        log "Aborting workflow..."
        ABORT_OUTPUT=$(wa robot workflow abort "$EXEC_ID" --reason "E2E test cleanup")
        log "Abort output: $ABORT_OUTPUT"
        
        ABORTED=$(echo "$ABORT_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.data.aborted')
        [ "$ABORTED" = "true" ] || { log "FAIL: abort failed"; exit 1; }
        log "✓ Workflow aborted successfully"
    fi
fi

log "✓ PASS: workflow lifecycle E2E test"
```

### Scenario: Events Unhandled Flag
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_events_unhandled.sh

set -euo pipefail
LOG_FILE="$ARTIFACT_DIR/events_unhandled.log"

log() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }

log "Testing --unhandled flag alias"

# Both flags should work
OUTPUT1=$(wa robot events --unhandled 2>&1)
OUTPUT2=$(wa robot events --unhandled-only 2>&1)

log "Output with --unhandled: $OUTPUT1"
log "Output with --unhandled-only: $OUTPUT2"

# Both should be valid JSON with same structure
echo "$OUTPUT1" | jq -e '.ok' || { log "FAIL: --unhandled output invalid"; exit 1; }
echo "$OUTPUT2" | jq -e '.ok' || { log "FAIL: --unhandled-only output invalid"; exit 1; }

log "✓ PASS: events unhandled flag E2E test"
```

- On failure: dump full output + stack trace to artifact directory
- Summary block at end: PASS/FAIL, elapsed_ms, key assertions

- [ ] All JSON schema validation tests pass
- [ ] All error code stability tests pass
- [ ] E2E workflow list test passes
- [ ] E2E workflow lifecycle test passes
- [ ] E2E events unhandled test passes

 {"id":"wa-01x","title":"Version compatibility check: detect incompatible wa/database combinations","description":"# Task: Version Compatibility Check

## Goal
Prevent users from accidentally running incompatible wa versions against their database.

## Scenarios
1. **Newer wa, older DB**: Migration needed (usually safe)
2. **Older wa, newer DB**: DANGEROUS - wa cannot read newer schema
3. **Major version jump**: May require manual intervention

## Implementation

### Database Version Tracking
Track schema_version, min_compatible_wa, created_by_wa, created_at in wa_meta table.

### Startup Check
On open, verify:
- current_wa >= min_compatible_wa (else: WaTooOld error)
- schema_version <= supported_schema (else: SchemaTooNew error)
- schema_version == supported_schema (else: MigrationNeeded)

### User-Facing Messages
Old wa + new DB: Clear error with upgrade instructions
New wa + old DB: Offer migration with backup

## Testing
- Unit tests: version comparison logic
- Integration: simulate version mismatches
- E2E: upgrade/downgrade scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- Old wa refuses to open newer DB with clear error
- New wa offers migration for older DB
- Version info tracked in database metadata
- Helpful upgrade instructions provided","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:00.500288986Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:47.047519125Z","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-storage","area-tests","area-ux","phase-1","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:47.047453772Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:45:44.971218440Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:58:19.090737493Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.11","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:55:36.964558253Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-01x","depends_on_id":"wa-rnf.5","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:55:36.203121200Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
